title: Missing zeroization of SHA-256 hash buffer in lora_l2.c
description:
In generate_eui64() at lora_l2.c:88-93, the local hash[32] buffer holds SHA-256(hwid). After memcpy(eui64, hash, 8), the remaining 24 bytes of hash are never zeroized.

Impact: While the hardware ID is not a cryptographic secret, leaving hash digests on the stack is a bad pattern. More critically, the same function pattern is used elsewhere with actual secrets (ipv6_addr.c with pubkey hashes). Inconsistent zeroization practices lead to developers copying the wrong pattern.

Fix: Add explicit_bzero(hash, sizeof(hash)) after the memcpy.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:96 (after memcpy)
